tasks.description: Export dialog UI with section toggles and presets. Create a new ExportDialog component that replaces the direct PDF download with a dialog offering toggleable report sections and 3 presets (Full Audit, Executive Summary, Gap Report). The dialog should list all available sections with checkboxes: Title Page, Executive Summary, Process Map, Gap Analysis, Cost Analysis, Journey Map, Journey Sentiment, Perspective Comparison, Prioritization Matrix, Tool Landscape, Improvement Recommendations, AI Insights. Each section shows enabled/disabled based on data availability. Presets auto-toggle relevant sections. Export button at bottom triggers PDF generation with selected sections. For this sub-task, build the dialog UI and state management only — actual PDF section renderers come in [2/4] and [3/4].
acceptance_criteria: New ExportDialog component exists (e.g., src/components/panels/export-dialog.tsx or src/components/export/export-dialog.tsx)
Dialog triggered from canvas-view.tsx export button (replaces or wraps existing direct PDF call)
12 section toggles rendered with checkboxes and labels
3 preset buttons (Full Audit, Executive Summary, Gap Report) that set toggle state
Sections that lack data show as disabled with muted text (e.g., Journey Map disabled if no journey tabs)
Export button at bottom calls a handler with the selected section config
Dialog uses Radix Dialog (existing ui/dialog.tsx) following codebase patterns
TypeScript types for ExportConfig (section booleans) defined in types/ or co-located

patterns_to_follow: Radix Dialog pattern from canvas-view.tsx Templates dialog
Checkbox state management — simple boolean record/object
learnings_relevant: Custom Radix DialogTitle wrapper breaks a11y check (BUG-023) — use DialogPrimitive.Title
apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly

tasks.special_instructions: This is [1/4] — build the DIALOG UI and export config types only. Do NOT implement actual PDF section renderers yet. The Export button handler should accept an ExportConfig object and for now just call the existing handleExportPdf as a passthrough. Wire into canvas-view.tsx by modifying the existing PDF export button to open this dialog instead of calling handleExportPdf directly. Also wire into journey-canvas-view.tsx if it has its own export button. Existing export functionality must still work (dialog → Export → same PDF as before).

tasks.description: Add active visual indicator to Tool Analysis toggle button. When analysis mode is active, the BarChart3 toggle button should have a more prominent active state — use brand color (--brand / teal) background similar to sidebar active states, not just accent-blue text.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle button has clear visual distinction between canvas mode and analysis mode
Active (analysis) state uses brand color or prominent background/outline
Inactive state is visually subdued (current default styling)
tasks.special_instructions: Small change — toggle button styling only. Look at existing showAnalysis state boolean and apply conditional className.

tasks.description: Fix stale sidebar improvements badge count after adding improvement from detail panel. Badge count loads once on mount and never refreshes. Add a refetch mechanism — simplest approach: refetch on window focus (visibilitychange event) or use a lightweight custom event emitted by createImprovementIdea.
acceptance_criteria: After adding an improvement from a detail panel, sidebar badge count updates without page reload
Implementation uses either visibility change listener, polling interval, or custom event pattern
No performance regression (avoid polling faster than every 30s if using interval)
Count still correct on initial load

patterns_to_follow: Existing sidebar fetch pattern for badge count (lines 62-74)

tasks.special_instructions: Simplest reliable approach: add a visibilitychange event listener that refetches the count when the tab becomes visible. This handles the case where user adds improvement in a panel (which might navigate/focus away) and comes back. Alternatively, a setInterval of 30-60s. Do NOT add a context provider for this — keep it simple.

shared_files.note: Slot 1 may create new types in src/types/export.ts — not a shared file.
